coredata.dc:title: Synthesis of NiO flower-like microspheres and their Electrochemical Performance
coredata.prism:copyright: Copyright © 2013 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.dc:creator: Zhang, Wenbao
Han, Yan
Zhang, Hong
Li, Shigang
Wang, Caihua
coredata.dc:description: We have successfully prepared β-Ni(OH)2 flower-like microspheres by the solvothermal method using 2-butanone and water mixture solvent for the first time. Then NiO flower-like microspheres were obtained by annealing the corresponding precursors at 300 °C. The flower-like microspheres were composed of many nanoplates. The thickness of nanoplates and the dimeter of flower-like microspheres were about 20 nm and 6 μm, respectively. For comparison, the sole β-Ni(OH)2 and NiO nanoplates were prepared in aqueous solution. Experimental results indicated that the as-synthesized NiO flower-like microspheres kept a relative high capacity retention during charge-discharge process, and their cyclic stability should be improved for potential application in lithium-ion batteries.
coredata.dcterms:subject: β-Ni(OH)2
NiO
flower-like microspheres
Electrochemical Properties

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: ACKNOWLEDGMENTS This work was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (No. 21201037 and 50903018) and Fuyang Normal College (FSB201301004, 2009FSKJ03, 2013HJJC03, 10040606Q49, KJ2011A209 and KJ2012Z305).

xocs:doc.xocs:rawtext: Synthesis of NiO flower-like microspheres and their Electrochemical Performance Wenbao Zhang, 1 2 Yan Han, 1 2 Hong Zhang, 1 2 Shigang Li, 2 Caihua Wang, 1 2 chw007@mail.ustc.edu.cn 1 School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Fuyang Normal College, Fuyang 236041, P. R. China School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering Fuyang Normal College Fuyang 236041 P. R. China School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Fuyang Normal College, Fuyang 236041, P. R. China 2 Anhui Provincial Key Laboratory for Degradation and Monitoring of Pollution of the Environment, Fuyang 236041, P. R.China Anhui Provincial Key Laboratory for Degradation and Monitoring of Pollution of the Environment P. R Fuyang 236041 China Anhui Provincial Key Laboratory for Degradation and Monitoring of Pollution of the Environment, Fuyang 236041, P. R.China We have successfully prepared Î²-Ni(OH)2 flower-like microspheres by the solvothermal method using 2-butanone and water mixture solvent for the first time. Then NiO flower-like microspheres were obtained by annealing the corresponding precursors at 300 Â°C. The flower-like microspheres were composed of many nanoplates. The thickness of nanoplates and the dimeter of flower-like microspheres were about 20 nm and 6 Î¼m, respectively. For comparison, the sole Î²-Ni(OH) flower-like microspheres by the solvothermal method using 2-butanone and water mixture solvent for the first time. Then NiO flower-like microspheres were obtained by annealing the corresponding precursors at 300 Â°C. The flower-like microspheres were composed of many nanoplates. The thickness of nanoplates and the dimeter of flower-like microspheres were about 20 nm and 6 Î¼m, respectively. For comparison, the sole Î²-Ni(OH)2 and NiO nanoplates were prepared in aqueous solution. Experimental results indicated that the as-synthesized NiO flower-like microspheres kept a relative high capacity retention during charge-discharge process, and their cyclic stability should be improved for potential application in lithium-ion batteries.Keywords Î²-Ni(OH)2 NiO flower-like microspheres Electrochemical Properties References 1. simple-head.ce:title: Synthesis of NiO flower-like microspheres and their Electrochemical Performance

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Fuyang Normal College, Fuyang 236041, P. R. China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Fuyang Normal College, Fuyang 236041, P. R. China

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Anhui Provincial Key Laboratory for Degradation and Monitoring of Pollution of the Environment, Fuyang 236041, P. R.China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Anhui Provincial Key Laboratory for Degradation and Monitoring of Pollution of the Environment

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Anhui Provincial Key Laboratory for Degradation and Monitoring of Pollution of the Environment, Fuyang 236041, P. R.China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: We have successfully prepared β-Ni(OH)2 flower-like microspheres by the solvothermal method using 2-butanone and water mixture solvent for the first time. Then NiO flower-like microspheres were obtained by annealing the corresponding precursors at 300 °C. The flower-like microspheres were composed of many nanoplates. The thickness of nanoplates and the dimeter of flower-like microspheres were about 20 nm and 6 μm, respectively. For comparison, the sole β-Ni(OH)2 and NiO nanoplates were prepared in aqueous solution. Experimental results indicated that the as-synthesized NiO flower-like microspheres kept a relative high capacity retention during charge-discharge process, and their cyclic stability should be improved for potential application in lithium-ion batteries.
